appeal_letter:
Re: Appeal of Denial for Inpatient Detoxification, dates of service 05/18/2026 to 05/21/2026

Dear Aetna Appeals Reviewer,

I am writing to appeal the denial of coverage for Inpatient Detoxification requested by my provider for the period of 05/18/2026 to 05/21/2026.

The denial letter states that, based on Aetna's Clinical Policy Bulletins (CPBs) and the ASAM (American Society of Addiction Medicine) Criteria, the clinical information did not demonstrate severe or complicated withdrawal symptoms (such as Delirium Tremens or seizures) requiring 24-hour medical nursing and monitoring, that my vital signs are stable, and that I do not have severe co-occurring medical conditions. The letter concludes that I could be safely treated at a lower level of care such as an Ambulatory (Outpatient) Detox program.

I respectfully ask that this decision be reconsidered in light of the clinical circumstances. The clinical record reflects a ten-year history of heavy daily alcohol use. The record also reflects that an attempt to quit resulted in severe tremors, profuse sweating, and visual hallucinations documented in the emergency room, along with a CIWA score of 18, described as moderate-to-severe. The provider has noted a high risk of progression to seizures based on prior withdrawal history. These findings appear directly relevant to the criteria cited in the denial regarding the severity of withdrawal and the risk of complications.

I ask that Aetna re-review the request using the complete clinical information, including the documented CIWA score of 18, the emergency room findings of tremors, sweating, and visual hallucinations, and the provider's assessment of high seizure risk based on withdrawal history. I request that the denial be overturned and that the requested inpatient detoxification be authorized for the dates of service noted above.

Please let me know if any additional documentation would assist in your review.

Not legal or medical advice. Draft assistance only.

Sincerely,
[Member Name]
[Member ID]
[Contact Information]
missing_evidence_checklist: Emergency room records documenting tremors, profuse sweating, visual hallucinations, and the recorded CIWA score of 18
Provider letter of medical necessity explaining seizure risk and rationale for inpatient versus ambulatory detox
Documentation of prior withdrawal history supporting elevated seizure risk
Vital sign records and any monitoring data from the relevant period
History of the ten-year heavy daily alcohol use as documented by the treating provider
Member identification number, plan information, and the denial reference number
